dimensionDescriptions.multi_source_fusion: Integrate identity, endpoint, network, email, and SIEM evidence into one defensible analytic thread; explain where sources corroborate each other and where they conflict.
dimensionDescriptions.narrative_coherence: Write a staged end-to-end storyline (access, foothold, movement, objective, response) and explicitly mark assumptions versus directly observed facts.
dimensionDescriptions.alternative_hypotheses: Present at least one credible alternative explanation, identify the strongest supporting evidence for it, and justify why it is currently less likely than your primary hypothesis.
dimensionDescriptions.detection_gap: Name the single most consequential visibility or detection failure that amplified impact, and explain why this gap mattered operationally.
dimensionDescriptions.prioritization: Justify why your selected action is highest leverage now, including expected risk reduction, timing, and dependency constraints.
dimensionDescriptions.containment_strategy: Describe immediate containment actions and sequencing, including what happens first, what follows, and the decision trigger for each step.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_preservation: Explain how containment choices affect forensic artifact retention and how you will preserve critical evidence while reducing active risk.
dimensionDescriptions.stakeholder_communication: Communicate conclusions in executive-ready language: clear business impact, decision relevance, and minimal jargon with necessary technical precision.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Tie risk level to likely impact, affected assets/processes, exposure duration, and adversary opportunity rather than indicator count alone.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Tie confidence level to corroboration strength, source reliability, and unresolved ambiguity; explain why confidence is not higher or lower.
dimensionDescriptions.uncertainty_collection: Specify 1–2 highest-yield collection actions, what result each action could return, and how that result would increase, decrease, or reverse confidence.
dimensionDescriptions.tradeoff: Explain the tradeoffs among speed, disruption, visibility, and long-term resilience for the response path you recommend.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary (audience: leadership)
reportSections.template: In plain language, summarize what most likely happened, current institutional risk, and the highest-priority decision leadership should authorize now. Keep this concise and decision-focused.

reportSections.title: Situation Overview and Assumptions
reportSections.template: Distinguish confirmed facts from assumptions, state key telemetry limitations, and note which assumptions most affect your conclusion.

reportSections.title: Intrusion Narrative (end-to-end lifecycle, with uncertainty noted)
reportSections.template: Reconstruct the likely lifecycle from initial access through impact using explicit stage transitions; label uncertain transitions and explain why they remain uncertain.

reportSections.title: Evidence Synthesis (at least 6 evidence items grouped by source type)
reportSections.template: Group at least 6 artifacts by source type (email, identity, endpoint, network, SIEM) and explain how each group supports or challenges your narrative.

reportSections.title: Primary Hypothesis and Alternatives (at least one alternative)
reportSections.template: Defend the primary hypothesis and include at least one plausible alternative with the key reason it is currently less likely.

reportSections.title: Risk Assessment (impact x likelihood, critical assets/processes)
reportSections.template: Assess impact and likelihood explicitly, identify critical assets/processes at risk, and justify the final risk level with concrete evidence.

reportSections.title: Confidence Rationale and What Would Change It (top 1–2 collection actions)
reportSections.template: State confidence level and rationale, then identify the top 1–2 collection actions and the specific outcomes that would change your confidence.

reportSections.title: Containment and Mitigation Plan (sequenced steps, tradeoffs, evidence preservation)
reportSections.template: Provide a sequenced plan of immediate actions, include operational tradeoffs for each step, and explain how evidence preservation will be maintained during containment.

reportSections.title: Detection/Visibility Improvement (specific gap + recommended fix)
reportSections.template: Identify the most consequential detection/visibility gap and recommend a concrete technical or process fix with expected effect on future detection quality.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion (next 24–72 hours and longer-term next steps)
reportSections.template: Close with an executive-ready action plan for the next 24–72 hours plus strategic improvements to reduce recurrence and improve response readiness.

gapRules.prompt: Integrate at least 3 telemetry types (for example identity, endpoint, and network) into one narrative chain and explain how they corroborate or conflict.
Provide a clear staged storyline from initial access to objective/impact, and explicitly label assumptions at each uncertain transition.
Add at least one plausible alternative hypothesis and state why it is less likely given the strongest conflicting evidence.
Justify why your chosen action is highest leverage now compared with at least one other reasonable option.
Explicitly address the containment-versus-evidence tradeoff and state how key artifacts will be preserved while response actions proceed.
Name the most consequential visibility failure that amplified risk and provide a concrete remedy to prevent recurrence.
State explicit confidence rationale tied to evidence quality, corroboration limits, and unresolved unknowns.
Specify 1–2 collection actions and the exact result from each action that would most change your current conclusion.
